title: michelia citrata
intro: Michelia citrata, also known as the lemon-leaf magnolia or Michelia alba, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, characterized by its white flowers and leaves with a distinctive lemon fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and alkaloids. The health benefits of Michelia citrata include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and alleviate digestive problems. Michelia citrata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine preparations.
